Package.Name: Scripting
Package.Summary: Execute complete JavaScript through the repository's bounded ScriptRunner and inspect return values, runtime types, limits, and structured failures.

Fields.ExpectResultHelp: When enabled, null or undefined is reported as a structured missing-result failure.

Result.Title: Execution result
Result.Empty: Run a script to see its returned value or structured failure.

Tips.Title: Why this matters
Tips.Isolation: Every request receives a fresh Jint engine, so scripts do not share mutable runtime state.
Tips.Bounds: Timeout, statement count, recursion, source size, memory, outer queue, and output are bounded independently.
Tips.ReturnValue: The final JavaScript expression becomes the returned value. A declaration alone usually evaluates to undefined.
Tips.Failures: Expected JavaScript and limit failures remain structured data rather than escaping as host exceptions.
Tips.Capabilities: Production hosts must expose every scalar value or delegate capability explicitly. Nothing ambient is available.

Purpose.Title: Purpose
Purpose.Body: Pocok.Scripting provides a narrow, host-controlled JavaScript boundary for automation and extension scenarios without exposing ambient CLR access.

QuickStart.Title: Quick start
QuickStart.Body: Write a complete script and leave the value you want to inspect as the final expression. The sandbox invokes the real ScriptRunner from the repository.

Results.Title: Results
Results.Body: Successful executions return the final JavaScript value. The showcase formats scalar, array, and object results deterministically and never exposes the captured exception object.

Bounds.Title: Execution bounds
Bounds.Body: ScriptRunner applies timeout, statement, recursion, source-length, memory, and cancellation limits. The showcase adds its own queue, input, and output bounds around that package contract.

Capabilities.Title: Explicit capabilities
Capabilities.Body: Hosts may register approved scalar values and delegates through ScriptBinding. Complex CLR objects and ambient interop remain unavailable.

Imports.Title: Imports and modules
Imports.Body: The package can resolve neutral comment imports from an explicit module source. This public playground focuses on direct complete scripts so no storage-backed module catalog is implied.

Concurrency.Title: Concurrency
Concurrency.Body: ScriptRunner is instance-based and safe for concurrent callers because each request creates a fresh engine. Showcase runs remain isolated by scope, cancellation source, result channel, and progress channel.

Production.Title: Production notes
Production.Body: Treat scripts as untrusted input, keep limits conservative, authorize every delegate capability, avoid secrets in diagnostics, and do not mistake in-process bounds for operating-system isolation.

Snippet.ScriptTitle: Complete script with a return value

Assist.Object: Return an object literal as the final expression.
Assist.Reduce: Reduce an array to one value.

Boundaries.Title: Boundaries
Boundaries.NoClr: No ambient CLR or reflection access.
Boundaries.NoIo: No filesystem, network, browser, or service-provider access.
Boundaries.FreshEngine: A fresh engine and script state for every run.
Boundaries.Bounded: Bounded source, timeout, statements, recursion, memory, queue, and output.

Source.Title: Source documentation
Source.Body: Repository documentation for the scripting package contract, capabilities, imports, and security boundaries.

arithmetic.Name: Function return value
arithmetic.Description: Define a function and return the triangular number of 12 from the final expression.

object-result.Name: Object and array result
object-result.Description: Return an object containing aggregate and transformed array values.

string-result.Name: Template string
string-result.Description: Return a JavaScript template string from a complete script.

missing-result.Name: Missing result
missing-result.Description: End with a declaration while a result is required and inspect the structured failure.

syntax-error.Name: Syntax failure
syntax-error.Description: Run invalid JavaScript and inspect its safe diagnostic location.

recursion-limit.Name: Recursion limit
recursion-limit.Description: Trigger the configured recursion boundary without affecting the host process.
